blocks.content: The porch steps were the first real test. Kit went first, testing each one — third had a soft spot, he stepped to the outer edge. Turned and showed the others where to walk.
The front door was more than unlocked — slightly open already, a black gap where the frame had warped.
He pushed the door open. It swung inward on a groan that went through the whole frame, and Kit stepped in and stopped and let his eyes adjust.
The entry hall. Staircase at the end. Doorways left and right. The floor was dark wood, thick with settled dust.
Kit bounced his weight lightly on the boards beneath him.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Please don't bounce in an abandoned house.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: And if it isn't, we'll know by the sound you make going through it.
He moved left into the living room. The others followed, spreading slightly but staying in sight.
